summary_one_line: A well-formed iTXt chunk whose translated-keyword field starts with a non-zero byte before length-end, so the L2796 NUL-scan loop runs and exits false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An iTXt chunk that survives the compression-info gate at buffer[prefix_length+1] (==0 or (==1 && buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE)) so control reaches the third loop at L2796; the translated-keyword region must contain at least one non-zero byte within `length` so the loop body executes and the false (loop-exit) side is taken at L2796.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog reaches the L2796 loop and exits it (avg hitcount on branch winner=23, resolved 10/10); I2SRandReplace can substitute the byte values the compression-info comparison expects (buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE) so the chunk passes the L2777-2779 gate, letting the third NUL-scan loop run to completion.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 with avg hitcount on branch loser=0 and stays blocked 9/10; its random havoc rarely hits the exact compression-info byte the gate compares against, so control diverts to the L2853 'bad compression info' else and never reaches L2796.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the equality CMP guarding the iTXt compression-info gate and splices the logged comparand into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing a gate-passing chunk that naive's blind havoc cannot reliably produce.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same structurally-valid iTXt chunk that reaches the L2796 translated-keyword NUL-scan loop and exits it on the false side; no comparison-solving is required, only that the input region around the translated-keyword offset contains a non-zero byte before `length`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount on branch winner=6); its CtxHook indexes each edge by call-context, so the iTXt-handler edges reached via the chunk-dispatch path register as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate iTXt seeds that naive discards and giving energy to mutants that progress through the prefix loops to L2796.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 (avg duration blocked loser=21.80h); its plain edge-bucket feedback already saw the iTXt loop edges from shallower chunks, so context-blind coverage gives no new-bucket reward for partially-advanced iTXt seeds and they are not retained long enough to mutate to the L2796 exit.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folding the caller chain into the edge-map index) creates fresh coverage credit for the iTXt loop edges reached via the chunk dispatcher, preserving and energizing the intermediate seeds needed to walk prefix_length past the translated-keyword field; naive's bare-edge feedback offers no such credit.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false (loop-exit) side of the third NUL-scan loop over the translated-keyword field at pngrutil.c:2796.
Reaching L2796 requires passing the compression-info gate that compares buffer[prefix_length+2] against PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE.
Inputs that fail the compression-info gate divert to the bad-compression-info else and never reach L2796.
cmplog wins over naive at this branch with prob_div 0.90 and a large hitcount divergence on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=23  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive resolves only 1/10 and stays blocked 9/10 at this branch.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CMP buffer and substitutes one operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive at this branch via the ctx_coverage technique with prob_div 0.80.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 at this branch with positive on-branch hitcount.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive's feedback is a bare per-edge hit-count bucket with no context indexing and no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 4055 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 1576443411134968` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 8 of the 9-level ancestor chain, which under the current build indicates an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in the cmplog winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic iTXt-handler harness where the compression-info gate byte is fixed/constant (no input-derived equality CMP) shows cmplog NOT outperforming naive at reaching the L2796 loop-exit, indicating I2S substitution was not the enabling factor.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the L2777-2779 compression-info equality is the specific CMP that I2SRandReplace solves is inferred from source structure; the lineage dash row confirms an I2S find in the chain but does not prove it targeted that exact comparand.
